meta.title: Royal Palace of Cambodia - History, Visitor Guide & Travel Information
meta.description: Explore Royal Palace of Cambodia in Phnom Penh. Discover its history, architecture, visiting information, cultural significance and a practical travel guide.

hero.title: Royal Palace of Cambodia
hero.subtitle: Ancient Royal Landmark · Phnom Penh, Cambodia

rows.label: Address
rows.value: Samdach Sothearos Blvd (3), Phnom Penh, Cambodia

about.title: About Royal Palace of Cambodia
paragraphs: The Royal Palace of Cambodia is one of Phnom Penh's most important cultural landmarks. Built in the 19th century, it serves as the official residence of the King of Cambodia and represents traditional Khmer architecture combined with influences from historical Southeast Asian royal design.

The walled compound sits on the west bank of the Tonlé Sap River and contains ceremonial halls, royal residences, gardens and sacred temples. It remains an active royal site and a living symbol of Cambodian heritage.

More than a monument, the palace is a working institution: state ceremonies, royal audiences and religious rituals continue here. For visitors, it offers a window into the art, religion and history that shaped modern Cambodia.

timeline.title: History Timeline
timeline.subtitle: From royal residence to national symbol

events.title: Phnom Penh becomes capital
events.desc: Phnom Penh is established as the capital of the Kingdom of Cambodia, setting the stage for a royal seat.

events.title: Palace complex construction begins
events.desc: King Norodom I begins construction of the royal palace compound on the west bank of the Tonlé Sap River.

events.title: Expansion and preservation
events.desc: Successive monarchs expand and restore the buildings, blending Khmer craftsmanship with European and Thai influences.

events.title: Cultural landmark & heritage site
events.desc: The palace serves as the official residence of the King and one of Cambodia's most visited cultural attractions.

architecture.title: Architecture Highlights
architecture.intro: The compound showcases traditional Khmer design enriched with decorative Thai and European influences.

items.name: Throne Hall
items.nameLocal: ព្រះរាជដំណាក់
items.desc: The central ceremonial hall used for coronations and royal receptions, crowned by a tiered golden spire.

items.name: Silver Pagoda
items.nameLocal: វត្តប្រាក់
items.desc: Also known as Wat Preah Keo, its floor is paved with more than 5,000 silver tiles and it houses Buddha statues in gold, crystal and emerald.

items.name: Royal Buildings
items.nameLocal: អាគាររាជវាំង
items.desc: A collection of pavilions, ministries and residences used for state functions and royal ceremonies.

items.name: Khmer Architectural Style
items.nameLocal: រចនាបថខ្មែរ
items.desc: High roofs, ornate nagas and vibrant frescoes reflect classical Khmer craftsmanship.

visitorGuide.title: Visitor Guide
visitorGuide.durationNote: Best in the morning to avoid midday heat and larger crowds.
tips: Visit early to avoid the heat
Photography is allowed in most outdoor areas
Allow extra time for the Silver Pagoda

ticket.title: Entrance Information
ticket.notes: Prices and visiting rules may change. Please verify with the official ticket office before your visit.

dressCode.title: Dress Code
dressCode.intro: The Royal Palace is an active royal and religious site. Visitors are expected to dress respectfully.

items.title: Shoulders covered
items.desc: Wear tops that cover the shoulders; sleeveless shirts are not permitted.

items.title: Respectful attire
items.desc: Avoid overly revealing clothing — this is a sacred royal compound.

items.title: Photography
items.desc: Allowed in most courtyards; avoid photographing guarded interiors.

transport.title: How to Get There
transport.intro: The palace sits in central Phnom Penh along the riverfront, easily reached from most parts of the city.

byAir.title: From Phnom Penh Airport
byAir.body: Phnom Penh International Airport (PNH) is about 10 km from the palace. Taxis and ride-hailing apps connect the airport to the city center.

byTrain.title: From the City Center
byTrain.body: The palace is a short tuk-tuk ride or walk from the riverside and most central hotels.

byCar.title: By Tuk-Tuk / Taxi
byCar.body: Tuk-tuks and taxis are the most common way to reach the palace from anywhere in the city.

gallery.title: Photo Gallery
gallery.caption: The Royal Palace compound, its gardens and the Silver Pagoda.

nearby.title: Nearby Attractions
nearby.intro: Within walking distance of the palace, you can explore more of Phnom Penh's heritage.

food.title: Local Food Experience
food.intro: Phnom Penh's riverside is rich with Khmer cuisine, from street stalls to traditional restaurants.

items.name: Khmer Cuisine
items.desc: The foundation of Cambodian food: rice, freshwater fish, lemongrass and kaffir lime.

items.name: Riverside Cafés
items.desc: Relaxed spots along Sisowath Quay for coffee, smoothies and people-watching.

items.name: Night Market Food
items.desc: Evening markets near the palace offer grilled skewers and local snacks.

reviews.title: Visitor Experience Summary
tips: Weekends and mornings can be busy
Outdoor areas get very hot midday

times.name: Morning
times.desc: Soft light and fewer crowds make for clean compositions.

times.name: Golden Hour
times.desc: Warm light turns the golden roofs and spires a deep amber.

bestTime.title: Best Time to Visit
bestTime.intro: Cambodia has a tropical climate; the cool dry season is the most comfortable for sightseeing.

seasons.note: Cool and dry — the best time to visit.
Hot and dry; plan an early-morning visit.
Green rainy season; fewer crowds but afternoon showers.

items.q: Where is the Royal Palace of Cambodia?
items.a: On Samdach Sothearos Blvd, by the Tonlé Sap River in central Phnom Penh.
Yes — shoulders and knees must be covered, as it is an active royal site.

items.q: How much is the entrance fee?
items.a: Prices may change; please check the official ticket office for current information.
